title: arenaria serpyllifolia subsp. macrocarpa
intro: Arenaria serpyyllifolia subsp. macrocarpa, also known as sea sandwort, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the carnation family (Caryophyllaceae). It has been traditionally used in medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to reduce pain and prevent infection. The plant is said to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation and relieving digestive issues, as well as being used as a treatment for respiratory problems such as bronchitis and coughs. Arenaria serpyllifolia subsp. macrocarpa can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
